    "Activity O (Trim carpentry) is delayed by 2 workdays due to crew shortage.",
    "Activity O is on the critical path, but shares the final inspection predecessor constraint with Activity P (Flooring).",
    "Activity P has 1 workday of total float relative to Activity O's original finish date.",
    "A 2-day delay to Activity O consumes its 1-day buffer relative to Activity P, resulting in a net 1-day delay to the project completion date."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Authorize overtime or reallocate carpentry crew members to recover the 1-day critical path delay.",
    "Reschedule the final inspection (Activity Q) out by 1 workday if recovery is not possible."
  ],
  "rationale": "The critical path runs through A-B-C-CC-D-E-F-I-J-K-L-M-N-O-Q (42 days total). Activity P (Flooring) takes 1 day and starts after N (day 39), finishing on day 40, which gives it 1 day of float before Q starts on day 41. Activity O (Trim carpentry) takes 2 days, starting on day 39 and finishing on day 41. A 2-day delay to Activity O extends its duration to 4 days, meaning it finishes on day 43 instead of day 41. This pushes the final inspection (Activity Q) to day 43, causing a net project delay of 1 workday.",
  "references": [
    "CPM Scheduling Standards"
  ]
